body.title: Unified memory node format: pilot one table with ID/METADATA/TRIGGERS/TAGS/DATA_PAYLOAD
body.result: tags + triggers columns on clan.memories; m058 migrations; 2921 rows backfilled; _derive_tags/_derive_triggers helpers; 4 tests
body.description:
Every persistent object in the system — tickets, memories, goals, habits, palace nodes, channel messages — shares the same outer shell:

  { ID, METADATA: {type, ...}, TRIGGERS, TAGS, DATA_PAYLOAD }

TAGS at the outer shell is structural/routing (Granny routes on it, evaluator fires on it, fast-indexed). TAGS inside DATA_PAYLOAD is semantic/content (domain-specific, type-specific meaning). Queries can target either layer or both: memory.tags contains foo vs memory.all contains foo.

TRIGGERS at the outer shell unifies what are currently scattered mechanisms: ticket gate, goal salience threshold, habit activation condition, memory retrieval salience. A single trigger evaluator fires any node when its conditions are satisfied — it doesn't know ticket from goal from habit.

This is the 'compiled inference' foundation: each node type gets a prebuilt view (e.g. SELECT * FROM tickets becomes a view over the unified table filtered by metadata.type = TICKET with pre-indexed access patterns). Consumers keep their existing query shape; the unification is underneath.

clan.memories is already closest to this shape. Pilot: promote tags out of metadata JSON into a top-level column, add triggers as a top-level jsonb column, verify node_registry lookup still works for calved payloads. One table only.

Gate on follow-on: after 12+ node types are live in the unified format and search effectiveness is validated (can we constrain searches meaningfully across type boundaries?), roll out to remaining tables.

**Affected files:** clan.memories schema migration, cortex.py (read/write path), any query that touches metadata->>'tags'
**Design rules:** no SQLite; Postgres only; existing consumers get views so their query shape does not change
**Scope boundary:** IN — clan.memories schema change (tags + triggers columns), cortex.py updated, prebuilt view for backward compat; OUT — migrating other tables (that is the follow-on gated ticket)
**Completion criteria:** clan.memories has top-level tags (jsonb array) and triggers (jsonb) columns; cortex.py reads/writes both; existing tag queries via metadata still work via a migration shim; at least one node type uses TRIGGERS field meaningfully; node_registry lookup unaffected
